meta.title: Servicio — A friendly desktop supervisor for your dev services
meta.description: Keep Laravel queues, Python workers, Node scripts — any command — alive, auto-restarted on crash, cron-scheduled, with live logs and CPU/memory graphs, from a calm control-room desktop app.

hero.title: Keep your dev services alive.
hero.subtitle: Servicio supervises your long-running developer processes — Laravel queues, Python workers, Node scripts, cron jobs, any command — restarted on crash, started on login, streaming logs and metrics, all from a calm control-room GUI.
hero.note: Universal macOS build today · Linux (systemd) supported · Windows on the roadmap

problem.title: A drawer full of forgotten terminals.
problem.body: Every developer ends up with a pile of php artisan queue:work, python worker.py, npm run dev, cron jobs and one-off scripts — each in its own terminal tab, each forgotten until it silently dies. Servicio is a small native desktop app plus a headless daemon that supervises all of them: keeps them running with restart-on-crash backoff, starts them on login with no sudo, and shows you live logs, CPU and memory graphs, and per-group rollups. No YAML by hand, no tmux gymnastics, no "is it still up?" — just a dashboard.

features.title: Everything you need to supervise.
features.subtitle: One calm dashboard for every long-running process on your machine.

items.title: Supervision
items.body: Auto-restart with exponential backoff, restart counters, graceful stop, and crash surfacing — the failure reason lands in the worker log, not the void.

items.title: Three run modes
items.body: Daemon (N always-on instances), Scheduled (cron or interval, with overlap policy), and Batch (run N times).

items.title: Autodetect
items.body: Scan a project folder and get ready-to-run suggestions from six detectors: Laravel, Procfile, crontab, VS Code tasks.json, Node and Python.

items.title: Groups & tags
items.body: Organize workers into groups with a folder view and drill-in, label them with tags, and filter the dashboard by tag.

items.title: Live telemetry
items.body: Per-instance CPU and memory sampling, fleet-wide and per-group rollups, and rolling sparklines.

items.title: Bulk actions
items.body: Start, stop, or restart a whole group in a single click.

items.title: Full CRUD
items.body: Create with a wizard, edit any worker, delete, and import or export your whole config as JSON.

items.title: Native & cross-platform
items.body: A universal macOS .dmg today, Linux via systemd, with a self-updating engine and optional Sentry error reporting.

showcase.title: See it in action.
showcase.subtitle: A control-room aesthetic that stays out of your way.

shots.dashboard_dark: Live dashboard with fleet CPU and memory graphs
shots.dashboard_light: The same dashboard in light mode
shots.groups: Per-group stats with bulk start, stop and restart
shots.wizard: The new-worker wizard with framework autodetect

architecture.title: A small Rust core, a native shell.
architecture.body: Servicio is a Rust cargo workspace plus a Tauri desktop app. The GUI talks to a headless daemon over a 0600 Unix socket with token auth; the daemon supervises every worker and persists them in SQLite. The same daemon answers the servicio CLI, so you can drive it from the terminal too.

rows.core: Process spawning, supervision, restart and backoff, run modes, metrics.
rows.ipc: The framed JSONL Frame protocol shared by daemon and clients.
rows.daemon: The long-lived supervisor: SQLite-backed worker store plus socket server.
rows.cli: servicio — a thin terminal client over the same protocol.
rows.detect: Folder scanners that propose workers (Laravel, Node, Python, …).

install.title: Get started in minutes.
install.macos_body: Download the universal .dmg from the latest release, drag Servicio to Applications, then right-click → Open on first launch (the build is currently unsigned).
install.linux_body: Build from source; the daemon installs as a systemd --user unit. Packaged Linux artifacts are on the roadmap.
install.cli_body: The same daemon speaks to the servicio CLI:

footer.tagline: A friendly desktop supervisor for your long-running developer services.
footer.built: Built with Rust, Tauri, and React.
